Our Story

Long BeforeThere Was a Club.

It started with two friends, a standing tee time, and no agenda.

Twenty years of rounds carried them through new jobs, growing families, and countless ordinary Tuesdays. Somehow, every round ended the same way: nobody quite ready to call it a day.

East | West is the place they kept wishing existed. Where the round doesn’t end. It just moves indoors.

2009Early Days
2012Rainy Rounds
2017The Circle Grew
2019Regulars
2023Kesin's First Swing
TodayStill Showing Up
A Living Timeline

How It All Came Together

Early in their friendship, weekday afternoons were easy to come by. Careers were just beginning, golf didn’t require much planning, and nobody was in much of a hurry to leave afterward. The rhythms of East | West were already there. They just didn’t have a name yet.

Seattle looked a little different then. So did Belltown. One round had a habit of turning into dinner, another drink, and an afternoon that slipped into evening.

Click a photo to reveal the story

Why East | West?

People often ask why we chose the name.

For Shankar, it begins with growing up between two worlds. Home in Pennsylvania felt distinctly Indian. His grandparents lived with the family, Telugu and Tamil filled the house, and mornings began with prayer. Outside the front door, he was a fairly ordinary central Pennsylvania kid who captained his high school tennis team and worked hard in school. Inside, the measure of things changed. Success and status mattered less. Food, family, faith, generosity, and conversation mattered more. Visits to the clubs his family belonged to in India later gave those same values a physical place, where work, family, and friendship naturally existed side by side.

Ryan’s upbringing looked different, but it led him toward many of the same ideas. The rhythm of his life was always organized around sport. Coaching, the weekend game, and becoming a regular somewhere taught him that people rarely become a community all at once. It happens by returning to familiar places, sharing experiences, and slowly becoming part of something bigger than yourself.

Over the years, they realized they weren’t describing two different ideas. They were describing the same human need through different experiences. One had learned it around a family table and in the clubs he visited in India. The other had found it on courts, in locker rooms, and on golf courses. East | West is our attempt to build a place that carries a little of both. Not a recreation of either world, but a place where the best parts of each can continue to shape everyday life.

Inside the Club

You’ve met the people. Now meet the place.

The story is only half of what we’re building. Explore the spaces, rituals, and everyday moments that bring East | West to life.
